questions.stem: For the signal flow graph shown in Fig. Q1 (two forward paths P1=G1G2G3 and P2=G4, with feedback loops involving H1 and H2), find the transfer function C/R.
common_errors: Missing the non-touching loop pair (L1L3) in the graph determinant delta
Incorrect cofactors delta1/delta2 — failing to check which loops touch each forward path
Sign error on loop gains (H1 and H2 feedback signs)
questions.solution_method: Identify two forward paths (P1=G1G2G3, P2=G4) and three loops (L1=-G2H1, L2=-G1G2G3H2, L3=-G4H2). Compute graph determinant Delta=1-(L1+L2+L3)+(L1L3) and cofactors Delta1=1, Delta2=(1+G2H1). Apply Mason's gain formula C/R=(P1*Delta1+P2*Delta2)/Delta.

questions.stem: For a transfer function T(s) = alpha/(s^2 + 2s + alpha), find the sensitivity S_alpha^T for alpha=2 at omega=0.1 rad/s.
common_errors: Differentiating T w.r.t. alpha incorrectly — forgetting alpha appears in both numerator and denominator
Not substituting s=jw before computing the magnitude
Arithmetic error in the magnitude: missing the sqrt(4+w^2) factor in numerator
questions.solution_method: Compute S_alpha^T = (dT/dalpha)*(alpha/T). Differentiate T to get S_alpha^T = s(s+2)/(s^2+2s+alpha). Substitute s=jw and alpha=2, then evaluate magnitude at w=0.1 to get approximately 0.1.

notes: Single-page quiz, 6 marks total, 12-minute duration (date 10.02.2026). Marking annotations visible: 1M per sub-step. Q2 final answer |S|≈0.1 confirmed in solution.
